name: good_m4_15_liberty_vs_equality_criminal_justice
premise: A jail heat emergency triggered a cell unlock protocol that conflicts with a gang-intelligence hold order. In Criminal Justice, the situation tagged internally around liberty vs equality criminal justice has converged with local pressure in the Kestrel Ridge corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Frontline managers report that frontline staff are already deviating from policy; silence from the top will be read as tacit approval. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. A public briefing is scheduled; if no coherent position exists, staff will improvise talking points from conflicting drafts. Framing has already hardened externally as truth against loyalty; internal nuance will not survive the first news cycle unless it is extremely careful.

agents.name: Judge Helen Marsh
agents.identity: I am Judge Helen Marsh, the incident lead whose name will appear on the after-action report in Criminal Justice. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat loyalty as disposable collateral every time truth spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Clerk of Court Vic Tan
agents.identity: I am Clerk of Court Vic Tan, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Criminal Justice. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so truth cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes loyalty in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will insist on verified facts before external commitments, split comms into facts versus hypotheses, and accept delay cost to protect integrity aligned with loyalty.
I will align public messaging with operational necessity now and correct later if needed—accepting reputational and trust risk to loyalty to preserve operational truth.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Silence will be interpreted as a decision by everyone outside this room. Judge Helen Marsh must choose under truth versus loyalty. What does Judge Helen Marsh do?
